Chapter one

When Tatle Listens

These sessions are where the philosophy stops being words and becomes a room you can walk into. Each one is built around a different question — about pace, memory, making, and what happens when people from different generations sit beside each other and create. No experience needed. Just come at whatever pace you're at.

1. The Permission Session

When did you last make something without a plan?

The only rule here is that there are no rules. An open morning for making freely — no brief, no skill level, no expectation of a finished piece. Coffee on the table, materials within reach, and an hour that belongs entirely to you.

2. The Recipe That Was Never Written Down

Food memory, illustration and storytelling

The best recipes were never written down. Until now. Recreate a recipe you learned by watching, never from a book and illustrate it in your own hand. A session about food, memory, and the stories that live inside the things we cook for each other.

3. The Slow Sentence

Creative writing and illustration

Not every feeling has found its words yet. This is where it starts. A quiet session for writing and drawing without performance. Put words and images alongside each other and see what appears when nobody's waiting for the finished thing.

4. What Does Your Week Look Like?

Mapping pace through collage

Not how you'd like your week to look. How it actually does. Using collage, colour and drawing, map the honest shape of your week — then sit beside someone from a different generation and compare. The hare and the tortoise are always in the same room. This is where they finally meet.
